title: `DATE_SUB` gives wrong result for near zero date
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```
select DATE_SUB('1000-01-01', INTERVAL 1000 YEAR);
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

```
mysql> select DATE_SUB('1000-01-01', INTERVAL 1000 YEAR);
+--------------------------------------------+
| DATE_SUB('1000-01-01', INTERVAL 1000 YEAR) |
+--------------------------------------------+
| 0000-01-01                                 |
+--------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.06 sec)
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

```
mysql> select DATE_SUB('1000-01-01', INTERVAL 1000 YEAR);
+--------------------------------------------+
| DATE_SUB('1000-01-01', INTERVAL 1000 YEAR) |
+--------------------------------------------+
| 0000-00-00                                 |
+--------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, event_date TEXT NOT NULL);
SELECT DATE(event_date, '-1000 years') FROM test_dates WHERE id = 1;
key_bug_features: Incorrect result from DATE_SUB for near zero date
